Package component.card

Class Card

java.lang.Object
component.card.Card
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
public class Card extends Object implements GridIndexable, Tippable
The Card class represents an individual entity within the game grid.

Each card carries a Suit, a numerical value, and a Material. It implements GridIndexable for spatial tracking and Tippable to provide contextual information when hovered by the user.

  • Constructor Details

    • Card

      public Card()
      Default constructor. Creates an Ace of Spades made of Plastic.

      Warning: Do not use this to manually inject cards into the game. Use GameLevel.addCard() instead to ensure proper registry.

    • Card

      public Card(Card card)
      Copy constructor. Creates a new card with properties identical to the source.
      Parameters:
      card - The card to clone.

    • Card

      public Card(Suit suit, int value, Material material)

    • Card

      public Card(Suit suit, int value, Material material, GridPos gridPos)

  • Method Details

    • getSuit

      public Suit getSuit()

    • setSuit

      public void setSuit(Suit suit)

    • getValue

      public int getValue()

    • setValue

      public void setValue(int newValue)
      Sets the card value with circular wrapping logic.

      Uses a mathematical modulo to ensure values outside the 1-13 range wrap around. For example, setting a value of 14 results in 1 (Ace), and 0 results in 13 (King).

      Parameters:
      newValue - The desired card value.

    • getMaterial

      public Material getMaterial()
      * Returns the material composition of the card.
      Returns:
      The Material type currently applied to this card.

    • setMaterial

      public void setMaterial(Material material)
      Updates the card's material and adjusts health accordingly.

      Material.GLASS cards are initialized with 3 health (breaking after 2 interactions), while other materials default to -1 (invulnerable/standard).

      Parameters:
      material - The new material to apply.

    • getHealth

      public int getHealth()
      * Returns the current health (durability) of the card.
      Returns:
      The health value. A value of -1 typically represents an indestructible state for non-fragile materials.

    • setHealth

      public void setHealth(int health)
      Updates the card's current health (durability) while enforcing a logical lower bound.

      This method uses Math.clamp to ensure the health value remains within a valid range. The value -1 is reserved as a sentinel indicating indestructibility (standard for Plastic or Metal materials).

      For fragile materials like GLASS, this value will typically be positive and decrement as the card interacts with modifiers, eventually leading to the card's destruction if it hits 0. * @param health The new health value to assigned to this card.

    • getGridPos

      public GridPos getGridPos()
      * Retrieves the current position of the card on the game grid.

      Implementation of GridIndexable.getGridPos().

      Specified by:
      getGridPos in interface GridIndexable
      Returns:
      The GridPos representing the card's X and Y coordinates.

    • setGridPos

      public void setGridPos(GridPos point)
      * Updates the card's location on the grid, enforcing level boundaries.

      Implementation of GridIndexable.setGridPos(GridPos). The coordinates are clamped between (0, 0) and the maximum dimensions defined in GameLevel.

      Specified by:
      setGridPos in interface GridIndexable
      Parameters:
      point - The target GridPos for the card.

    • isBlocking

      public boolean isBlocking()
      Returns whether this card blocks other entities.
      Specified by:
      isBlocking in interface GridIndexable
      Returns:
      Always true, as card stacking is prohibited.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      * Generates a string representation of the card for debugging.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A string containing the class name, suit, and value.

    • isEquivalent

      public boolean isEquivalent(Card card, boolean checkMaterial)
      Compares two cards for logical equivalence.

      Note: Standard equals() is not overridden to avoid hash-collision issues in collections where card identity (memory address) matters more than value.

      Parameters:
      card - The card to compare against.
      checkMaterial - Whether the material type must also match.
      Returns:
      true if the cards are equivalent.

    • isEquivalent

      public boolean isEquivalent(Card card)
      Compares two cards for logical equivalence. Does not compare materials.

      Note: Standard equals() is not overridden to avoid hash-collision issues in collections where card identity (memory address) matters more than value.

      Parameters:
      card - The card to compare against.
      Returns:
      true if the cards are equivalent.

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Generates a descriptive tooltip for the UI.
      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Returns:
      A Tooltip containing the card's name, material, and value details.
